rows.notes: Single centered word at top of page, legible cursive 'Soluta'. Clear header with no amounts.

Second centered line below title appears to name officers (Rector, Scholarius & Officiarijs). Handwriting slightly ambiguous on first word ('Gno' vs 'Tho' etc.), chose reading that best matches visible loops.
Small marginal scribble at left introducing the first bracketed group. Handwriting abbreviated and faint; interpreted as 'Test. Not.' as a section label.
Description shows left bracket and word 'Stipendium' clearly. Amount columns aligned to ruling lines; pounds '20', shillings '6', pence '8' are crisp and valid.

Amount '26 5 0' clear in columns. Description handwriting somewhat cramped; read as 'Augment Rech: Southweington & Morton' — several village/place letters uncertain (notably 'Southweington'), hence moderate confidence.

Monetary columns read '33 6 8' clearly. Description reads like 'Stipend' and a following word interpreted as 'the Sheirs' or 'the Shiers'; word uncertain due to cursive but context (repeated 'ditto' later) supports this reading.
Description is abbreviated 'Ditto' and repeated on the line. Amount '50 0 0' is clear and well-aligned.
Another 'Ditto' line; amounts '34 6 8' are well-formed and aligned with ruling lines.
Left margin label introducing next bracketed group. Handwriting is abbreviated/faint; read conservatively as 'Test. Annu?' indicating an annual/testament section.
Bracketed 'Stipendium' similar to earlier. Amount '28 9 6' is clear in columns; digits unambiguous.

rows.description: Pro silus part. de Kildington Augment
rows.notes: Amount '20 6 8' clear. Description is in Latinized form and somewhat faded; read as 'Pro ... parte de Kildington Augment' — several letters unclear (e.g. 'silus/tilus'), chosen reading based on letter shapes and context.
Small margin note marking a new subsection; characters ambiguous. Transcribed conservatively as seen 'Test. D.B.B.'

Description 'Stipendium' bracketed. Amount columns read as '09 9 4' with a small handwritten '3/4' mark after the pence — interpreted as three farthings (0.75). Columns alignment supports pounds=9, shillings=9, pence=4¾.
Amount '33 6 8' clear. Description reads 'Pro 4ta parte de Kildington Augment' though the '4ta' and 'Kildington' letters are slightly cramped; context confirms this is a quarter-part entry.
Another 'Stipend'/'Sheirs' line. Amount '50 0 0' is clear and aligned. Description partly abbreviated but consistent with earlier similar lines.
Description 'Dividend: Ditto' readable. Amount '27 0 0' unambiguous in columns.
Left margin label introducing miscellaneous testamentary items. Handwriting a bit compressed; read as 'Test. Misc.'
Bracketed 'Stipendium' again. Amount '20 1 8' is legible and well-aligned.

This line is written as continuation under the bracket but shows no amount. Likely a sub-item description 'Pro Augment Recd de Loguither' (spelling uncertain). No monetary figures present on this row.
Centered heading for bursar payments. Handwriting readable though final letters a little stylized; transcribed as 'Soluta Bursarijs'.

rows.description: Jan. 27. 1759. Mag'r Upton pro Decret. Sept. Nat. 1757
rows.notes: Date and name line; amount in columns reads '62 1 7' with a small '1/4' mark after the pence — interpreted as quarter-penny (0.25). Description letters partly abbreviated (Mag'r, Decret.), but meaning clear.

rows.description: July 21. 1759 Mag'r Webber pro Decret Sept S.P. 1758
rows.notes: Amount '59 0 8' clear. Description is an abbreviated dated bursar item; 'Webber' and abbreviations (Mag'r, Decret) are legible though slightly cramped.

rows.description: Mag'r Upton pro Decret Sept Nat. 1758
rows.notes: Amount '78 11 5' is readable though the '11' shillings required careful alignment checking. Description similar to earlier Upton entry; date year present as 1758.
Centered header for scholars' recoveries or fines. Lettering is faint in parts; transcribed as 'Soluta Scholar Causieri' though last word uncertain.
Left date '1759' then 'Share of Clifton Henry fine' (several name letters uncertain). Amount '00 19 3' is clear in columns. Name 'Henry' read from cursive but could be slightly different.
Description reads like 'Widow Perk's' or 'Penk's' — chosen as 'Widow Penk's fine' though name uncertain. Amount '00 13 1' visible with a small '1/2' mark after the pence indicating halfpenny (0.5).
Name read as 'Henchard' (handwriting slightly ambiguous between 'Henchard'/'Henchens'). Amount '06 18 5' is clear; a small halfpenny symbol after pence interpreted as 0.5.
Centered heading for Mitchell's pensions; legible though ink lighter than surrounding text.
Clear short entry 'Bible clerk' with amount '03 0 0' well-aligned and unambiguous.
Centered heading 'Soluta Varia' (various payments). The heading is clear and distinct from other sections.

rows.description: Lloyd for surveying Yandor Timber May 12. 1759
rows.notes: Amount '00 11 6' clear. Description refers to 'Lloyd' and surveying; place/name 'Yandor' (or similar) is uncertain from the handwriting; date 'May 12. 1759' is legible.

rows.description: Half a Doe for ye Hall
rows.notes: Description reads as 'Half a Doe for ye Hall' (archaic spelling 'ye' visible). Amount '00 6 6' is aligned and clear.

rows.description: Collins Chandler in part (of a bill 38.16.11)
rows.notes: Amount '20 0 0' clear. Description 'Collins Chandler' and parenthetical reference to a bill '38:16:11' are legible though letters slightly compressed.

Amount columns read '14 14 5' with a small '3/4' after the pence — interpreted as three farthings (0.75). Description appears to be 'Allowed Drapers expenses' (word 'Drapers' uncertain; could be another trade name) — moderate confidence due to faintness.
Name and purpose 'John Day for Chapel' are readable. Amount '01 2 6' aligns well and digits are clear.

Bottom-right final total line. Numerals '584 18 4' are clear; small fractional mark after the pence interpreted as '3/4' (0.75). No textual label on the same line; treated as total because of horizontal rule and positioning.
